user.title: Sensors and consumers
user.description: Select the battery, solar, and house sensors, consumer switches, and battery capacity (kWh). Next: other numeric parameters (defaults are fine for most users), then two PV strings for the forecast.

data_description.battery_soc_sensor: Sensor that reports battery state of charge in percent (0–100). Example: sensor.solarman_battery_soc
data_description.battery_power_sensor: Sensor for battery power in W (positive = discharge). Example: sensor.solarman_battery_power
data_description.solar_production_sensor: Sensor for solar production power in W. Example: sensor.solar_production_power
data_description.house_consumption_sensor: Sensor for total house consumption in W. Example: sensor.house_power
data_description.consumer_switches: Switches or input booleans for heavy consumers (boiler, AC, pool, etc.). The order is the priority: first = turned on first when there is surplus, last = turned off first when saving. You can also add a group that contains these entities.

advanced.title: Advanced parameters
advanced.description: Numeric settings and optional helpers. Latitude and longitude for the solar forecast are taken from your Home Assistant home location automatically (you can still correct them later by reconfiguring if needed). Defaults match the previous single-step setup.

data.lights_to_turn_off: Devices to turn off in super-saving (optional)
data.recommended_to_turn_off: Recommended to turn off when battery low (optional)

data_description.forecast_pr: Performance ratio applied to the solar forecast (0–1 typical). Example: 0.85
data_description.max_battery_discharge_power_kw: Optional. Cap for max discharge power in kW (0 = use learned peak from battery power).
data_description.max_battery_charge_power_kw: Optional. Cap for max charge power in kW (0 = use learned peak from battery power).
data_description.inverter_size_kw: Optional AC output limit in kW for clipping checks. Use 0 to disable.
data_description.lights_to_turn_off: Optional. Devices (lights, switches, fans, etc.) to turn off automatically in super energy saving mode (e.g. when battery very low).
data_description.recommended_to_turn_off: Optional. Intermediate devices: when battery is low and daily forecast is short, the integration recommends turning these off (sensor exposes the list; no automatic turn-off).
data_description.battery_horizon_verbose_attributes: When enabled, Battery Runtime/Time To Full also expose edge timestamp, target SOC, and hourly projection attributes.

consumer_power_sensor.title: Consumer power sensor mapping
consumer_power_sensor.description: Consumer {consumer_index}/{consumer_total}: {consumer_entity_id}. Select an optional dedicated power sensor for this consumer, or leave empty to keep fallback behavior.

data_description.power_sensor_entity_id: Dedicated power sensor in W for this consumer.

strings.title: PV strings (panels) – installation angle, capacity
strings.description: Configure 2 PV strings for solar forecast. For each string: installed capacity (kW), installation angle – tilt (°), azimuth (°).

data_description.string_0_system_size_kw: Installed capacity of first string in kW. Example: 5
data_description.string_0_tilt: Panel tilt from horizontal in degrees (0–90). Example: 30
data_description.string_0_azimuth: Panel azimuth: 0 = South, -90 = East, 90 = West, ±180 = North. Example: 0
data_description.string_1_system_size_kw: Installed capacity of second string in kW. Example: 5
data_description.string_1_tilt: Panel tilt from horizontal in degrees (0–90). Example: 30

main.title: Sensors and consumers
main.description: Update battery, solar, and house sensors, consumer switches, and battery capacity (kWh). Next: other numeric parameters, then PV strings.

data_description.battery_soc_sensor: Sensor that reports battery state of charge in percent (0–100). Example: sensor.solarman_battery_soc
data_description.battery_power_sensor: Sensor for battery power in W (positive = discharge). Example: sensor.solarman_battery_power
data_description.solar_production_sensor: Sensor for solar production power in W. Example: sensor.solar_production_power
data_description.house_consumption_sensor: Sensor for total house consumption in W. Example: sensor.house_power
data_description.consumer_switches: Switches or input booleans for heavy consumers (boiler, AC, pool, etc.). The order is the priority: first = turned on first when there is surplus, last = turned off first when saving. You can also add a group that contains these entities.

telegram.title: Telegram (Bot API)
telegram.description: Send ops-log alerts to Telegram (direct API, not notify). Leave bot token empty to keep the current token. Enable bot commands only for chat IDs you list.

data.telegram_min_interval_sec: Min seconds between identical alerts (0 = off)

data_description.telegram_enabled: When off, no messages are sent and command polling idles.
data_description.telegram_bot_token: From @BotFather. Leave blank when saving to keep the existing token.
data_description.telegram_chat_ids: Numeric chat id(s), comma-separated. Same list is used for inbound command allowlist.
data_description.telegram_out_categories: MODE, ACTION, FORECAST, LEARN, SYSTEM — same taxonomy as the ops log.
data_description.telegram_events_denylist: Snake_case event names to never forward, e.g. consumer_turned_on, open_meteo_timeout
data_description.telegram_min_interval_sec: Per-entry dedupe window for identical alerts (same as sensor ring fingerprint).
data_description.telegram_commands_enabled: Uses getUpdates in the background; only listed chat IDs can use /status, /clear_alerts, etc.

advanced.title: Advanced parameters
advanced.description: Numeric settings and optional helpers. Latitude and longitude are kept from your existing configuration when valid; otherwise they fall back to the Home Assistant home location.

data.lights_to_turn_off: Devices to turn off in super-saving (optional)
data.recommended_to_turn_off: Recommended to turn off when battery low (optional)

data_description.forecast_pr: Performance ratio applied to the solar forecast (0–1 typical). Example: 0.85
data_description.max_battery_discharge_power_kw: Optional. Cap for max discharge power in kW (0 = use learned peak from battery power).
data_description.max_battery_charge_power_kw: Optional. Cap for max charge power in kW (0 = use learned peak from battery power).
data_description.inverter_size_kw: Optional AC output limit in kW for clipping checks. Use 0 to disable.
data_description.lights_to_turn_off: Optional. Devices (lights, switches, fans, etc.) to turn off automatically in super energy saving mode (e.g. when battery very low).
data_description.recommended_to_turn_off: Optional. Intermediate devices: when battery is low and daily forecast is short, the integration recommends turning these off (sensor exposes the list; no automatic turn-off).
data_description.battery_horizon_verbose_attributes: When enabled, Battery Runtime/Time To Full also expose edge timestamp, target SOC, and hourly projection attributes.

consumer_power_sensor.description: Consumer {consumer_index}/{consumer_total}: {consumer_entity_id}. Select an optional dedicated power sensor for this consumer, or leave empty to keep fallback behavior.

data_description.string_0_system_size_kw: Installed capacity of first string in kW. Example: 5
data_description.string_0_tilt: Panel tilt from horizontal in degrees (0–90). Example: 30
data_description.string_0_azimuth: Panel azimuth: 0 = South, -90 = East, 90 = West, ±180 = North. Example: 0

abort.reconfigure_required: Existing setup uses legacy consumer config. Please reconfigure the integration to define consumers with optional power sensors.

reset_consumer_learn.name: Reset learned consumer power
reset_consumer_learn.description: Clears learned per-consumer power. Learning resumes when consumers turn on in wasting mode.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Energy Manager config entry id. Leave empty to reset all Energy Manager instances.

clear_integration_alerts.name: Clear integration alerts
clear_integration_alerts.description: Clears the in-memory alert ring buffer (last-alert sensor).

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Energy Manager config entry id. Leave empty to clear all instances.
